epigraph.text: A good name is more desirable than great riches.
epigraph.quote: Mathematics is the art of giving the same name to different things.

content.text: {Mathematics is the art of giving the same name to different things.}{Henri Poincar\'e}

Functions deserve their own notation—a way to name them and 
communicate precisely about inputs and outputs.

Instead of writing $y = 3x + 2$, we can write:
\[
 f(x) = 3x + 2
\]
This is read "$f$ of $x$ equals $3x + 2$" or "$f$ at $x$ is $3x + 2$."

content.title: Function Notation
content.content:
The notation $f(x)$ represents the output of function $f$ when the 
input is $x$. The expression $f(x)$ is read "$f$ of $x$."

The notation $f(x)$ does **not** mean $f$ times $x$! It means 
"the function $f$ evaluated at $x$."

content.text: To find $f(a)$, substitute $a$ for every $x$ in the function rule.

We often use letters like $f$, $g$, $h$, $p$, or $q$ for functions. 
The input variable can also vary: $f(t)$, $g(n)$, $h(s)$.
The input can be any expression, not just a number.

content.title: Expression inputs
content.solution:
Replace every $x$ with the given expression:

1. $f(a) = 3a - 2$

2. $f(x + 1) = 3(x + 1) - 2 = 3x + 3 - 2 = 3x + 1$

3. $f(2x) = 3(2x) - 2 = 6x - 2$

content.title: The difference quotient
content.problem: Let $f(x) = x^2$. Find and simplify $\frac{f(x + h) - f(x)}{h}$.

content.text: This "difference quotient" is fundamental in calculus!
Sometimes we know the output and must find the input.

marginNotes.text:
I have summoned you by name; you are 
mine.

The beginning of wisdom is this: 
Get wisdom. Though it cost all you have, get understanding.

marginNotes.author: Leonhard Euler
marginNotes.text:
Mathematicians have tried in vain to 
this day to discover some order in the sequence of prime numbers.
